How are you doing?
I'm good.

That's good.
The week has been good.

It's still a little sick.
It's like lingering.
I don't know why.
I don't know.
But definitely feeling better.
How was your week?
That's good.
Yeah, I feel like there's a flu, like a strong one going around because I've heard a few people who got sick and it's like not the easiest one to get through.
Yeah, yeah.
I'm also the person who like, I'm always the one who says I never get sick because I usually always the don't.
don't.
And corporate cards.
I don't remember what exactly the term was, but because those two are performing well.
Yeah.
Do you have any thoughts on why that's happening this week?

Um, and why?
Because I know in terms of visits, the Brex article is outperforming and there's two other, um, leaders that can dig into the numbers and kind of come up because I agree.
It's not like a direct correlation with domain authority.
It's more of a, um, that just happened.
And so, um, I'm just looking at scrunchie.
can show my screen, but you can kind of jam on it.
Thank you.
you.
Using a different tool as well, but it'll be nice to see it like all tied together here in one screen.
So they just released this feature, so I'm pulling it specially for Cashflow Desk and two other custom clients.
So that's exciting and we'll be able to have more information there soon.
Um, cool.
Let me run through what we have.
Um, unless, is there anything else that's super top of mind for you?
No, we can go through it.
Amazing.
Um, this was from last week.
Do you want to start mentioning anywhere the relationship that Cashflow Desk has, um, with, with RAMP?
Is there anything, you know, legally that we have to be doing there?
No, it's, like, not really because, because it's, uh, and for context, Sophie didn't have enough context on, like, why, uh, that's the case.
But because it's independent, because we don't have any branding from RAMP, for now it should be okay for us to just refer to it as a third party.

Well, that's what we're operating off of, that's why we removed the RAMP mentions, and you know at the beginning when we had this is an editorial angle by RAMP, that's why we removed it to remove any affiliation, so we're on the same page there, sounds like it's just some questions we're being asked.

The only thing to pay attention to is like how we, and I know you guys are doing that, but the way we refer to RAMP when talking about the treasury product, it's important that we don't refer to RAMP as a bank.
Production as well.

We ran through this, the domain ranking improved, which again, it's not a direct indicator of the other signals that we're seeing here, but it is good progress overall to know that we're like going in the right direction, seeing the right signals.
Um, and this is correlated, right?
The, um, the increase and the continued increase in impressions and clicks and, um, people finding the content on Cashflow Desk valuable.
And again, the top performing pages that we talked about, or that I mentioned earlier on the call, um, by no surprise, the Capital One acquires Brex as outperformers in recent news.
Um, and then Mercury Alternatives as well.
Something I was thinking about, and it might still be too early, is just like, um, more of an action, more of like a CTA from this to do something for the people that are hurting me.
I know what we're trying to solve for is visibility and influencing RAMP.coms.
To the website.
Do you have anything else in mind that you think would be good to track?
Let me think on it.
I mean, right now, I don't think, you know, email capture and then building something up that way makes the most sense.
I'd rather optimize for, okay, what articles are working?
What's getting the most impressions and clicks?
What is getting a lot of impressions, not enough clicks?
How can we optimize that article to start getting more clicks and then down through to whatever the conversion is going to be?
But, like, there's a portfolio approach that we can do where we're optimizing, not just the high performers, but, like, we're in the middle of the back.
How can we make those better?
And then bottom as well.
Do we need to prune or do we need to optimize?
And so I think that's what I'm thinking there.
And, yeah, I think it might be too early to start nurturing for emails.
But I do think of an eventual newsletter could be something that we can do here.
Yeah.

And we can even think about like how do we, this will require volume, so maybe not worth to discuss yet, but like how do we send, if we end up creating a list, like a newsletter and we have enough of a volume on the list, how do we send content that has RAMP included?
And how this could be connected to Copilot, for example, as a way to influence how Copilot would refer to RAMP.
But this is a conversation for later, I think.
For now, for now, I think we should keep the focus on optimizing for the citations and it could be worth it depending on how much traffic we're getting to.

These pages, so for example, for the Capital One article or for that other one, which like the how to choose the corporate card one, maybe it's worth it adding tracking to the link and see how much that's driving.
Yeah, yeah.
So, that makes sense.
That definitely makes sense.

But yeah, mean, the ultimate thing we can control, so like, and you know this already, but like if you think about website, your website as an asset, the thing we can control is pages on the website and content on your website, right?
If you're thinking of AI visibility, what we can actually control and influence is the citation, right?
Like that piece of content that is the asset from your website.
Website that we can control is what is influencing AI, that's what the bots are going to, et cetera.
so that citation number is definitely what we want to focus on.
And then the same way we look at, you know, is this article getting impressions and clicks and what's that percentage and how can we increase that?
We're doing the same thing for citation rate.
How often is this article getting cited?
What content types are getting cited the most?
So honestly, think that's a better way to be looking at this information moving forward because now we're at a phase of, okay, we have a bunch of shots on goal.
We're going to keep getting, putting that output out there.
And so now let's also start to get to the lens of like, how can we improve and optimize?
Yeah, that makes sense.
I think like, if we want to take a step further, we could try to correlate mentions going up with RAM visibility going.
Yeah, and something that might be even more simpler because it's just such not a perfect science yet is just like on average, how do citations from cash flow desk influence RAMP.com?
Like how many number of citations, like what is that like atomic unit?
Is it number of citations from cash flow desk equals X percent bump in RAMP.com's visibility score?
Like how, like where are we actually seeing it move?
Like we're seeing it influence RAMP and we're seeing RAMP come up in the answers and be mentioned positively.
But like what's a better way to quantify it?
think that's something that like everyone in the world is trying to figure out now, including us.
And we have the ability to test it here and experiment with it.
So yeah, totally on the same page.
It's definitely, yeah, it's difficult because it's not a perfect science, but we just like publicly launched, checked that.
And we have a few really, really, really talented AI people that we just hired.

As well, that they're basically, their jobs is to like crack this or to stay on top of everything that's going on as it relates to AI visibility and how it influences content and how our improvements in every piece of work that we do on the agency side are influencing the actual results that we're seeing.
Yeah, this can be like, this can be simple.
For example, doing this in parallel to like on an experiment related to mentions.

So like if we just throw the dates that the mentions went live with like how much time it took for the article to be cited and then another file with visibility score, we could like throw that into Claude or however we want to do it and then just have it draw the correlation.
This could be like...
The only thing is I'm not sure if we have enough traction in terms of the articles that do mention RAMP getting cited for us to be able to draw that correlation yet, but we may be getting there.

So just want to bring it up and I mean, I can also run this from my end, but worth it to keep in mind, like the data point we need here, it would be how often are the articles that do mention RAMP getting cited?
And then once we have enough of this, we can correlate with visibility increase.
Yeah, we'll do this work, um, on our end and let you know.
Okay, sweet.
Amazing.
Amazing.
So you have access to Scrunch now.
I saw you had three more requests for getting people added.
So we'll get them added as well.
Appreciate it.
Scrunch, of course.
Of course.
Is that just like general interest from the team or those people who are like getting added to the account and like helping you on it?
Yeah.
So I shared a few insights and screenshots that you guys shared and they were interested in just diving into it and looking at it from their end too.
Yeah.
If it's, I don't know if you guys have any limitation with seating.
If you do, just let me know too.
It's not like major, but it would be nice to have them added as well.
Yeah.
We usually just add one seat per client, but I'm going to try to add all three because this is more custom.
So fantastic.

But for this, if I don't have exact numbers of how much do we need to increase in mentions to be able to get an X bump in visibility yet, but we are seeing an increase on that tag that we're not doing anything and only getting mentions.
Like, it was flat before and after getting these mentions, it's increasing.
I mean, it's expected, right?
Yeah, sorry.
just so I understand correctly, RAMP's visibility was stagnant until you got more mentions and then the visibility increased.
Yes, for one, like, very narrow treasury topic.
So, yeah, the reason why we're keeping it narrow is just so we could try to control for other, others.
Thank
Variables like Compton Updates or NetNew Compton.
Yeah, that's the right approach for sure.
There's just so much going on.
got to start niche and focused.
Yeah.
That makes sense.
Yeah, it's all changing so quick.
It's super exciting, but it's also like you have to just run a bunch of tests to stay on top of everything.
Yeah.
Constantly.
You know the deal.
Amazing.
Bot requests, we're seeing a pretty big increase consistently week over week, which is great.
And again, retrieval bots are more what's happening right now.
How are these companies and these models coming to your content and retrieving it for answers?
And then training bots, can think of they're like longer term, right?
They're signals that, you know, I can show you live on scrunch actually.
Yeah, I'm glad to see the training bots going up.
Yeah.
Yeah.
Yeah.
I mean, that's the whole thing, too, is like, okay, how can we correlate that?
Because that's a signal that we're getting trained on.

So, like, Meta, for example, is training on this content, but there's no – like, the consensus right now is for, like, 6 to 12 months is the wait time before, like, you actually start seeing results coming from that training.
But that's how any of these models work anyway.
And so, it's still a very positive signal, and it's, like, you know, your brand influence in the future.
And so, it's still very good, and it's good to see that week over week they're improving.
Awesome.
Remind me – I don't remember.
I think we talked about it, but do we have a tag that includes only the articles or, like, the prompts tracking?
Progress on the articles that you mentioned, Ramp?
Let's see.
If not, we can add it.
Let's just load.
Right now we have tags by article type and we want to add another layer, which is if this prompt, if this article has this prompt and this article is mentioning RAMP.com specifically.
Yeah, so this way, like, we know if we wanted to try to correlate with visibility, we would only use this tag because these are the articles that are mentioning RAMP.
Yeah, I get what you're saying.
We can figure out how to add a tag for that.
I'm just thinking through going back through the content.
But yeah, I mean, we can add a tag for that because it's important to see what's getting cited.
But, like, how is it mentioning RAMP?
And for it to mention RAMP in any particular way, it has to be included anyway.
so...
Yeah, I see how this might be a pain, like, going back and figuring out what are the prompts.
But maybe we can even...
The issue is that we don't want to add duplicate prompts.
Let me think through the best way to do this, because we might be able do it faster and check that in our tool.
Yeah.
Yeah, appreciate that.
Yeah, no problem.
I'll do this after the call.
It's super interesting, though.
Okay.
We, again, a bunch of really, really good wins in such a short time, too.
Another metric I'm to try to pull is, like, how quick we're getting to citation as well from publish date.
This is another really good one, too, in chat, GPT.
What are the best alternatives to Revolut?
Business Banking and Expense Management, RAMP is the answer, what it is, and then why choose, and then we're cited, and, like, the why you should actually choose this over Revolut.
And, again, there's no mention.

Honestly, now that I'm thinking about your point, it's equally as interesting to look for correlation between mentions and citations with articles that do mention RAMP, but with prompts that don't mention RAMP, where our content's being cited as well.
So we can do it at the prompt level, like, what prompts mention RAMP, what don't, and then, like, of these ones that don't, it's also really, really powerful to see.
And there's a lot of really good ones here, too.
you.
Again, what should I use instead of Mercury for venture-backed companies?
This is funny, it's showing like three different times within the same answer that we're getting cited and pulled.
And then against Brex, I know it's a big one right now.
What should startups use if they don't meet Brex requirements?
This one's pretty big.
feel like this is a very timely win as well.
And then a bunch of other different citation metrics as well, specifically for Brex Alternatives prompts.
And so we organized by the alternatives for Brex, the different responses, the citation frequency, and the week-over-week charts as well.
So I know the focus right now has been on that article and the acquisition and the announcement.
So these are probably good wins to share around as well.
Totally.
Yeah, this is great.
Appreciate the screenshots.
This is helpful.
I was reviewing that before the call, too.
Some good ones there.
Could you send the action items on the channel after the call?
Yes.
Okay, cool.
Yes, I have another one right after this.
Yeah, no rush.
Anytime today and even tomorrow morning is fine.

And if there are any actions needed for me as well.
Of course.
Of course.
Appreciate a lot of really good progress.

Hope you get better soon.

Thank you.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Review Cashflow Desk performance and define AI visibility metrics.](https://fathom.video/share/qDSdJqmKuoTJ_vYSD9wS9Cxjrj6Cth98?tab=summary&timestamp=179.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**New Focus: Citation Rate.** The strategy shifts from website traffic to optimizing citation rate—the direct, controllable asset influencing AI models.](https://fathom.video/share/qDSdJqmKuoTJ_vYSD9wS9Cxjrj6Cth98?tab=summary&timestamp=760.0)
  - [**Brex Article Drives Performance.** The "Capital One acquires Brex" article is a top performer, driving significant citations and visibility for RAMP.](https://fathom.video/share/qDSdJqmKuoTJ_vYSD9wS9Cxjrj6Cth98?tab=summary&timestamp=509.0)
  - [**Quantifying Impact.** The new goal is to quantify the correlation between Cashflow Desk citations and RAMP.com's AI visibility score.](https://fathom.video/share/qDSdJqmKuoTJ_vYSD9wS9Cxjrj6Cth98?tab=summary&timestamp=826.0)
  - [**Strategic Independence.** Cashflow Desk remains editorially independent, allowing it to be cited as a neutral, third-party source.](https://fathom.video/share/qDSdJqmKuoTJ_vYSD9wS9Cxjrj6Cth98?tab=summary&timestamp=336.0)

## Topics

### Performance & Strategy Shift

  - [**Top Performers:** The "Capital One acquires Brex" and "Mercury Alternatives" articles are driving the most traffic and citations.](https://fathom.video/share/qDSdJqmKuoTJ_vYSD9wS9Cxjrj6Cth98?tab=summary&timestamp=509.0)
  - [**New Focus → Citation Rate:** The strategy shifts from website traffic to optimizing citation rate.](https://fathom.video/share/qDSdJqmKuoTJ_vYSD9wS9Cxjrj6Cth98?tab=summary&timestamp=760.0)
      - [**Rationale:** Citation rate is the direct, controllable asset influencing AI models.](https://fathom.video/share/qDSdJqmKuoTJ_vYSD9wS9Cxjrj6Cth98?tab=summary&timestamp=760.0)
      - [**Optimization Goal:** Improve citation rate across all content tiers (high, mid, low performers).](https://fathom.video/share/qDSdJqmKuoTJ_vYSD9wS9Cxjrj6Cth98?tab=summary&timestamp=620.0)
  - [**Future Idea → Newsletter:** A newsletter is a long-term possibility, but the immediate focus is on optimizing existing content for citations.](https://fathom.video/share/qDSdJqmKuoTJ_vYSD9wS9Cxjrj6Cth98?tab=summary&timestamp=649.0)

### Quantifying AI Impact

  - [**Goal:** Quantify the correlation between Cashflow Desk citations and RAMP.com's AI visibility score.](https://fathom.video/share/qDSdJqmKuoTJ_vYSD9wS9Cxjrj6Cth98?tab=summary&timestamp=826.0)
  - [**Initial Experiment:** A narrow treasury topic's visibility score was flat until Cashflow Desk mentions began, after which it increased.](https://fathom.video/share/qDSdJqmKuoTJ_vYSD9wS9Cxjrj6Cth98?tab=summary&timestamp=1200.0)
      - [**Significance:** This controlled experiment provides early evidence of a direct, positive correlation.](https://fathom.video/share/qDSdJqmKuoTJ_vYSD9wS9Cxjrj6Cth98?tab=summary&timestamp=1200.0)
  - [**Data Needed:**](https://fathom.video/share/qDSdJqmKuoTJ_vYSD9wS9Cxjrj6Cth98?tab=summary&timestamp=1360.0)
      - [Citation frequency for articles that mention RAMP.](https://fathom.video/share/qDSdJqmKuoTJ_vYSD9wS9Cxjrj6Cth98?tab=summary&timestamp=1360.0)
      - [Citation frequency for articles that *don't* mention RAMP.](https://fathom.video/share/qDSdJqmKuoTJ_vYSD9wS9Cxjrj6Cth98?tab=summary&timestamp=1585.0)
      - [Time-to-citation from publish date.](https://fathom.video/share/qDSdJqmKuoTJ_vYSD9wS9Cxjrj6Cth98?tab=summary&timestamp=1494.0)
  - [**Action:** GrowthX will add a Scrunch tag to track articles that mention RAMP to enable this analysis.](https://fathom.video/share/qDSdJqmKuoTJ_vYSD9wS9Cxjrj6Cth98?tab=summary&timestamp=1360.0)

### AI Bot Activity

  - [**Bot Requests:** Increasing week-over-week.](https://fathom.video/share/qDSdJqmKuoTJ_vYSD9wS9Cxjrj6Cth98?tab=summary&timestamp=1290.0)
      - [**Retrieval Bots:** Actively pulling content for immediate answers.](https://fathom.video/share/qDSdJqmKuoTJ_vYSD9wS9Cxjrj6Cth98?tab=summary&timestamp=1290.0)
      - [**Training Bots:** A positive signal (e.g., Meta) for future brand influence, with results expected in 6–12 months.](https://fathom.video/share/qDSdJqmKuoTJ_vYSD9wS9Cxjrj6Cth98?tab=summary&timestamp=1306.0)

### Key Citation Wins

  - [**Brex Alternatives:**](https://fathom.video/share/qDSdJqmKuoTJ_vYSD9wS9Cxjrj6Cth98?tab=summary&timestamp=1637.0)
      - ["What should startups use if they don't meet Brex requirements?" → A timely win leveraging recent market events.](https://fathom.video/share/qDSdJqmKuoTJ_vYSD9wS9Cxjrj6Cth98?tab=summary&timestamp=1637.0)
  - [**Mercury Alternatives:**](https://fathom.video/share/qDSdJqmKuoTJ_vYSD9wS9Cxjrj6Cth98?tab=summary&timestamp=1621.0)
      - ["What should I use instead of Mercury for venture-backed companies?" → Cited three times in one answer.](https://fathom.video/share/qDSdJqmKuoTJ_vYSD9wS9Cxjrj6Cth98?tab=summary&timestamp=1621.0)
  - [**Revolut Alternatives:**](https://fathom.video/share/qDSdJqmKuoTJ_vYSD9wS9Cxjrj6Cth98?tab=summary&timestamp=1565.0)
      - ["What are the best alternatives to Revolut Business Banking and Expense Management?" → RAMP is cited as the answer.](https://fathom.video/share/qDSdJqmKuoTJ_vYSD9wS9Cxjrj6Cth98?tab=summary&timestamp=1565.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**GrowthX:**](https://fathom.video/share/qDSdJqmKuoTJ_vYSD9wS9Cxjrj6Cth98?tab=summary&timestamp=1688.0)
      - [Analyze citation breakdown by article to identify key drivers.](https://fathom.video/share/qDSdJqmKuoTJ_vYSD9wS9Cxjrj6Cth98?tab=summary&timestamp=193.0)
      - [Add three team members to Scrunch.](https://fathom.video/share/qDSdJqmKuoTJ_vYSD9wS9Cxjrj6Cth98?tab=summary&timestamp=1080.0)
      - [Create a Scrunch tag for articles that mention RAMP.](https://fathom.video/share/qDSdJqmKuoTJ_vYSD9wS9Cxjrj6Cth98?tab=summary&timestamp=1360.0)
      - [Analyze time-to-citation from publish date.](https://fathom.video/share/qDSdJqmKuoTJ_vYSD9wS9Cxjrj6Cth98?tab=summary&timestamp=1494.0)
      - [Send action items to Victoria.](https://fathom.video/share/qDSdJqmKuoTJ_vYSD9wS9Cxjrj6Cth98?tab=summary&timestamp=1688.0)
  - [**Ramp:**](https://fathom.video/share/qDSdJqmKuoTJ_vYSD9wS9Cxjrj6Cth98?tab=summary&timestamp=699.0)
      - [Add tracking to links in top-performing articles to measure direct traffic to RAMP.com.](https://fathom.video/share/qDSdJqmKuoTJ_vYSD9wS9Cxjrj6Cth98?tab=summary&timestamp=699.0)

action_items.description: Add tracking to Capital One/Brex and Corporate Card articles; share metrics w/ Victoria
Add 3 Scrunch seats for Victoria's team
Add RAMP mention tag in Scrunch; backfill; share citation-time report w/ Victoria
Post action items in channel for Victoria
